science.title: Hidden Solar Storms May Be Lighting Japan’s Skies With Massive Red Auroras
science.summary: Unusually tall red auroras over Japan may be revealing that some solar storms are stronger than scientists realize. A study published in the Journal of Space Weather and Space Climate reports that red auroras observed over Japan extended to altitudes of roughly 500-800 kilometers above Earth. science.title: Everyone thought these helmets were Roman until scientists uncovered the truth
science.summary: Researchers have solved a decades-old mystery by showing that a cache of 43 helmets found off the Spanish coast is medieval, not Roman. The remarkable discovery exposes a thriving weapons trade network that connected Mediterranean powers during a time of piracy, warfare, and growing demand for military equipment.

science.title: Scientists finally complete Schrödinger’s 100-year-old color theory
science.summary: Researchers have finally resolved a key problem in a 100-year-old theory of color, showing that the qualities we perceive in colors are intrinsic to the mathematics of color space itself. The discovery sharpens our understanding of human vision and could lead to more precise color technologies and visualizations.

science.title: The Strange “Spacetime Crystal” That Can Suddenly Turn Into a Black Hole
science.summary: A new mathematical breakthrough sheds light on how tiny black holes could emerge from critical states of spacetime. Black holes are often portrayed as cosmic giants, swallowing stars and shaping entire galaxies. But some of the most intriguing black holes predicted by physics could be far smaller than an atom. science.title: Vast Hidden Structure Discovered Under Miles of Ice in East Antarctica
science.summary: Scientists have discovered a colossal hidden structure beneath Antarctica that could reshape our understanding of the continent’s past and present. tech.title: Amazon’s Echo Hub gets a customizable new look and Ring’s AI features

medicine.title: Scientists finally crack an “undruggable” pancreatic cancer target and nearly double survival
medicine.summary: For decades, pancreatic cancer has been one of the most lethal cancers, with few effective treatment options. A new drug, daraxonrasib, targets the KRAS mutation that fuels most pancreatic tumors—something many scientists once thought couldn't be done. medicine.title: New treatment shows promise for patients with rare blood disorder
medicine.summary: A new treatment that involves growing a patient's immune cells and then infusing them back into their body has shown promise for people with the rare blood disorder aplastic anemia. medicine.title: Home blood pressure tests could prevent heart attack and stroke
medicine.summary: Allowing patients to measure and record their blood pressure at home has been linked to a drop in the risk of cardiovascular diseases, such as heart attack and stroke, shows a study recently published in the European Heart Journal–Digital Health. Patients who were able to self-monitor their blood pressure, with the dat

medicine.summary: Blood pressure management after thrombectomy for acute ischemic stroke may require a change in approach. medicine.title: New antibody may boost KRAS-targeted lung cancer treatment after resistance emerges
medicine.summary: An experimental antibody treatment that binds to a protein known as PCDH7 shrank tumors in preclinical models of non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), including those resistant to a targeted therapy, a study led by UT Southwestern Medical Center researchers showed. mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. space.title: NASA's ambitious Artemis 3 mission includes 3 giant rocket launches, 2 private moon landers and 1 big question: Can it all work together?
space.summary: NASA's Artemis 3 mission in 2027 looks to be one of agency's most complicated missions ever, with three giant rocket launches and two private moon lander prototypes that haven't been built yet.

space.title: NASA reveals Artemis 3 astronauts for critical moon lander test flight
space.summary: NASA named the astronauts who will launch on its Artemis 3 mission next year to test docking procedures in Earth orbit ahead of a planned moon landing in 2028.

astronomy.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers 'galaxy-killing' wind that may explain why some early galaxies lived fast and died young
astronomy.summary: A “galaxy-killing” wind driven by cosmic mergers may explain why many massive galaxies in the early universe stopped forming stars far earlier than expected, according to new JWST and ALMA observations.

astronomy.title: On The Hunt For Cosmic Dawn And The Universe’s Very First Stars
astronomy.summary: After decades of searches, cosmologists are within reach of finding cosmic dawn. A longtime observational cosmologist explains.

architecture.title: Wadhal subverts suburban architecture for home in west London
architecture.summary: Architecture studio Wadhal has completed Wade House in London, using textured brickwork, pared-back fenestration and concealed guttering to put a subtly contemporary spin on typical suburban architecture. architecture.title: MACH designs red town hall in Spain to have "warm and welcoming presence"
